title: Add Prometheus metrics and ServiceMonitor sub-reconciler
description:
# GitHub Issue #122: Add Prometheus metrics and ServiceMonitor sub-reconciler

**Description**: Register custom Prometheus collectors in the Keystone operator: reconciliation duration histograms per sub-reconciler, reconciliation error counters by condition type, fernet/credential key rotation age gauges (last rotation timestamp), and database sync job duration/failure counters. Add a ServiceMonitor CR emitted by a sub-reconciler or Helm template so Prometheus auto-discovers the operator metrics endpoint on port 8080.

**Motivation**: The operator has a metrics port (8080) configured in the Helm chart but emits no custom metrics. For production operational visibility, operators need to monitor reconciliation health, detect degraded conditions, and alert on key rotation failures. Without metrics, operators are flying blind.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go`
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Reconciliation duration histogram registered per sub-reconciler
- [ ] Error counter by condition type incremented on failures
- [ ] Fernet/credential key age gauges expose last rotation timestamps
- [ ] ServiceMonitor template added to Helm chart (opt-in via values.yaml)
- [ ] Metrics endpoint verified accessible on port 8080
- [ ] Grafana dashboard JSON or mixin provided as documentation

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/122

stories.title: Cluster operator observes reconciliation health via Prometheus
stories.want: per-sub-reconciler duration histograms and error counters exported by the Keystone operator (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: I can detect slow or failing reconciliation phases (e.g. FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady) and alert before the OpenStack control plane degrades
criteria: GET to http://<operator-pod>:8080/metrics returns a 200 with at least one keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds_* sample (CC-0089, REQ-001)
Each sub-reconciler listed in subConditionTypes contributes a labelled time series with label sub_reconciler=<name> (CC-0089, REQ-001)
Histogram buckets cover the expected latency range (10ms, 50ms, 100ms, 250ms, 500ms, 1s, 2.5s, 5s, 10s, 30s) so p50/p95/p99 are computable by PromQL (CC-0089, REQ-001)
A reconciliation that returns an error increments keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total{sub_reconciler=<name>,condition_type=<type>} exactly once (CC-0089, REQ-002)
A reconciliation that returns ctrl.Result{} with err == nil does NOT increment the error counter (CC-0089, REQ-002)

stories.title: Security operator monitors fernet/credential key rotation freshness
stories.want: Prometheus gauges that expose the age in seconds since the last successful fernet and credential key rotation for every Keystone CR (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: I can configure alerts when rotation has not occurred within the configured rotation_interval, catching silent CronJob failures before credentials become stale
criteria: keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds{keystone=<name>,namespace=<ns>,key_type=fernet} is emitted with the age derived from the forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at annotation on the staging Secret (CC-0089, REQ-003)
keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds{keystone=<name>,namespace=<ns>,key_type=credential} is emitted with identical semantics (CC-0089, REQ-003)
When no rotation has ever completed (annotation absent or unparseable), the gauge is NOT set — PromQL absent(...) is the operator alert signal (CC-0089, REQ-003)
Gauge value refreshes on every successful reconcile of the parent Keystone CR so age tracks wall-clock correctly (CC-0089, REQ-003)
Deleting a Keystone CR removes the associated time series within one reconcile of the delete event (CC-0089, REQ-004)

stories.title: SRE tracks database sync job health for upgrade risk management
stories.want: counters for db_sync Job completions and failures plus a histogram of db_sync Job duration (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: I can alert on repeated db_sync failures during upgrade windows and track drift between expected and actual migration duration
criteria: keystone_operator_db_sync_total{keystone=<name>,namespace=<ns>,result=succeeded|failed} increments exactly once per Job terminal state transition (CC-0089, REQ-005)
keystone_operator_db_sync_duration_seconds histogram records the wall time from Job create to terminal condition (CC-0089, REQ-005)
Transient in-progress reconciles do NOT increment the counter — only the transition to Complete or Failed does (CC-0089, REQ-005)
Labels match other operator metrics (keystone, namespace) for consistent PromQL joins (CC-0089, REQ-005)

stories.title: Cluster operator opts into ServiceMonitor scraping via Helm
stories.want: a ServiceMonitor template shipped by the keystone-operator Helm chart, gated by monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: Prometheus Operator auto-discovers the metrics endpoint without me authoring ServiceMonitor YAML by hand, while clusters without prometheus-operator-crds installed are unaffected
criteria: helm template with monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true produces a ServiceMonitor of apiVersion monitoring.coreos.com/v1 (CC-0089, REQ-006)
helm template with monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=false produces zero ServiceMonitor objects (CC-0089, REQ-006)
The rendered ServiceMonitor selects the operator Service by selectorLabels and scrapes port 'metrics' at path '/metrics' (CC-0089, REQ-006)
values.schema.json validates monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled as a boolean and monitoring.serviceMonitor.interval as a duration string (CC-0089, REQ-006)
The default value of monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled is false — opt-in, never opt-out (CC-0089, REQ-006)

stories.title: Keystone operator developer instruments a new sub-reconciler
stories.want: a single well-documented instrumentation entry point that wraps any sub-reconciler with timing and error counting (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: adding a future sub-reconciler (e.g. CC-0090) requires exactly one line change and cannot forget to emit metrics
criteria: A helper func (r *KeystoneReconciler) instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, name, fn) (ctrl.Result, error) exists and is used by every sub-reconciler invocation in Reconcile (CC-0089, REQ-007)
The helper is covered by a unit test that records call counts against a test registry (CC-0089, REQ-007)
docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md contains a section 'Metrics Instrumentation' explaining the helper and naming convention (CC-0089, REQ-009)
A unit test asserts the metric names and label sets match the documented contract to prevent accidental renames (CC-0089, REQ-007)

stories.title: Operator reads a reference doc for the exported metrics
stories.want: reference documentation listing every metric name, type, labels, and buckets exported by the Keystone operator (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: I can author dashboards and alerting rules against a stable contract
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-operator-metrics.md exists and lists: metric name, type (counter/histogram/gauge), labels with cardinality notes, bucket layout, semantic meaning (CC-0089, REQ-009)
Doc includes the ServiceMonitor enablement snippet for Helm values (CC-0089, REQ-009)
Doc links the Grafana dashboard JSON artefact shipped with the release (CC-0089, REQ-010)
Doc is linked from docs/guides/observability.md (CC-0089, REQ-009)

stories.title: Operator imports a starter Grafana dashboard
stories.want: a ready-to-import Grafana dashboard JSON committed alongside the operator (CC-0089)
stories.so_that: I get a working visualisation the day I enable ServiceMonitor scraping, without having to design panels from scratch
criteria: operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json exists and parses as valid Grafana dashboard JSON (CC-0089, REQ-010)
Dashboard contains at minimum: reconcile duration p95 per sub-reconciler, reconcile error rate per condition type, key rotation age per Keystone, db_sync duration and failure count (CC-0089, REQ-010)
Dashboard uses only metrics defined in this feature — no undefined or speculative series (CC-0089, REQ-010)
docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md shows the import command (CC-0089, REQ-011)

requirements.description: The operator SHALL expose a keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds histogram labelled by sub_reconciler for every sub-reconciler invocation on the main Reconcile path
requirements.rationale: Per-phase latency is the primary signal for detecting degraded reconciliation; aggregating under a single controller-level metric loses the ability to pinpoint which phase is slow

scenarios.name: Successful sub-reconciler observation
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys returns (ctrl.Result{}, nil) after 42ms
scenarios.then: one observation is recorded in keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds{sub_reconciler="FernetKeys"} with value approximately 0.042
and_then: the 0.05 bucket counter increments by 1
the 0.1, 0.25 ... +Inf bucket counters also increment by 1 (cumulative histogram)

scenarios.name: Errored sub-reconciler still observed
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase returns (ctrl.Result{}, errors.New("boom")) after 120ms
scenarios.then: an observation is still recorded in the duration histogram for sub_reconciler="Database"
and_then: duration is measured via defer so early return does not skip emission

scenarios.name: Bucket layout matches documented contract
scenarios.when: the histogram is registered
and_then: the documented contract in docs/reference/keystone-operator-metrics.md matches the registered buckets exactly

requirements.description: The operator SHALL increment keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total with labels sub_reconciler and condition_type exactly once per errored sub-reconciler invocation
requirements.rationale: Counting errors by condition type lets Prometheus distinguish transient failures (e.g. API throttling during NetworkPolicyReady) from load-bearing failures (DeploymentReady) and drives differentiated alerting

scenarios.name: Error increments counter
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns a non-nil error
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total{sub_reconciler="Deployment",condition_type="DeploymentReady"} increments by 1
and_then: no other sub_reconciler label increments

scenarios.name: Success does not increment
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns (ctrl.Result{}, nil)
scenarios.then: the error counter for that label pair is unchanged

scenarios.name: Non-zero Result without err is not an error
scenarios.when: a sub-reconciler returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 5*time.Second} with err == nil
scenarios.then: the error counter is NOT incremented
and_then: requeue-without-error is the normal steady-state backoff signal and must not pollute the error rate

scenarios.name: Parallel group error attributed to the correct sub-reconciler
scenarios.when: reconcileParallelGroup returns an error from the CredentialKeys member
scenarios.then: the error counter for sub_reconciler="CredentialKeys" increments by 1 while FernetKeys and NetworkPolicy counters are unchanged

requirements.description: The operator SHALL emit a keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds gauge for each (keystone, namespace, key_type) triple when the rotation-completed-at annotation is present and parseable
requirements.rationale: Silent CronJob failures are the primary risk for fernet/credential rotation; exposing the age allows absent() and age > threshold alerting

scenarios.name: Staging Secret with annotation updates gauge
scenarios.when: the fernet staging Secret carries annotation forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at="2026-04-22T12:00:00Z" and the reconciler runs at 2026-04-22T12:05:00Z
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds{keystone="<name>",namespace="<ns>",key_type="fernet"} is approximately 300

scenarios.name: Staging Secret absent does not emit gauge
scenarios.when: no staging Secret exists for a Keystone CR (first-rotation steady state)
scenarios.then: the gauge for that (keystone, namespace, key_type) triple is NOT set
and_then: PromQL absent(keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds{...}) is the alerting signal

scenarios.name: Unparseable annotation does not emit gauge
scenarios.when: the staging Secret's annotation is "not-a-timestamp"
scenarios.then: the gauge is NOT set and a Warning event RotationAnnotationInvalid has already been emitted elsewhere

scenarios.name: Credential key gauge tracks independently
scenarios.when: fernet rotated 10 minutes ago but credential never rotated
scenarios.then: key_type="fernet" gauge emits age ≈ 600; key_type="credential" gauge is NOT set

requirements.description: The operator SHALL remove per-CR metric time series when a Keystone CR is deleted
requirements.rationale: Without deletion cleanup, metric cardinality grows monotonically for every historical CR ever reconciled, eventually OOM-ing the operator pod

scenarios.name: Delete event removes rotation age gauge
scenarios.when: reconcileDelete runs to completion for keystone="foo" in namespace "bar"
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds series with keystone="foo",namespace="bar" are deleted from the registry
and_then: this MUST happen after the finalizer is released, not inside the timed reconcile path

scenarios.name: Histogram and counter series are NOT deleted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is deleted
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds and keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total series are retained (labels do not include keystone name)
and_then: these metrics are labelled only by sub_reconciler — they have bounded cardinality independent of CR count

requirements.description: The operator SHALL emit db_sync Job metrics: a duration histogram and a terminal-state counter
requirements.rationale: db_sync is the highest-risk step in the reconcile flow during upgrades; Job-level observability is distinct from sub-reconciler-level timing because the Job lifecycle spans multiple reconciles

scenarios.name: Successful Job increments succeeded counter
scenarios.when: the db_sync Job transitions to Complete=True
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_db_sync_total{keystone="<name>",namespace="<ns>",result="succeeded"} increments by 1
and_then: keystone_operator_db_sync_duration_seconds records one observation from Job.CreationTimestamp to Job.Status.CompletionTime

scenarios.name: Failed Job increments failed counter
scenarios.when: the db_sync Job transitions to Failed=True
scenarios.then: keystone_operator_db_sync_total{keystone="<name>",namespace="<ns>",result="failed"} increments by 1
and_then: duration histogram records observation using Job.CreationTimestamp to time of last condition transition

scenarios.name: In-progress Job does not increment counter
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase observes a Job with Complete=False,Failed=False (still running) across ten reconcile passes
scenarios.then: neither counter nor histogram is updated on any of the ten passes
and_then: emission must be guarded by a condition transition, not by every reconcile observing the Job

requirements.description: The keystone-operator Helm chart SHALL ship an opt-in ServiceMonitor template gated by monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled
requirements.rationale: The operator runs on clusters that may or may not have prometheus-operator-crds installed; unconditional ServiceMonitor emission would break installation on CRD-less clusters

scenarios.name: Opt-in renders ServiceMonitor
scenarios.when: helm template is invoked with --set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true
scenarios.then: exactly one object of kind ServiceMonitor, apiVersion monitoring.coreos.com/v1 is produced
and_then: its spec.selector.matchLabels equals selectorLabels of the operator Service
its spec.endpoints[0].port equals "metrics"
its spec.endpoints[0].path equals "/metrics"
its spec.endpoints[0].interval defaults to 30s

scenarios.name: Default renders no ServiceMonitor
scenarios.when: helm template is invoked with default values
scenarios.then: zero objects of kind ServiceMonitor are produced
and_then: this guarantees installation succeeds on clusters without prometheus-operator-crds

scenarios.name: Schema validation rejects wrong types
scenarios.when: helm template --set monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled="yes" is invoked
scenarios.then: helm fails with a values.schema.json validation error
and_then: enabled must be a boolean, interval must match a duration pattern

requirements.description: The operator SHALL provide a single instrumentation helper instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, name, fn) used by every sub-reconciler invocation in Reconcile
requirements.rationale: Centralising instrumentation prevents future drift (a new sub-reconciler forgetting to emit metrics) and keeps metric semantics uniform

scenarios.name: Helper records duration and returns inner result verbatim
scenarios.when: instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, "Deployment", fn) is called where fn returns (ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 10*time.Second}, nil)
scenarios.then: the return value equals (ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 10*time.Second}, nil) byte-for-byte
and_then: duration histogram has exactly one observation for sub_reconciler="Deployment"

scenarios.name: Helper increments error counter on err != nil
scenarios.when: the inner fn returns (ctrl.Result{}, err)
scenarios.then: error counter increments with condition_type inferred from a registered lookup table
and_then: the lookup table is built from subConditionTypes plus the three const-declared condition types

scenarios.name: Helper is panic-safe
scenarios.when: the inner fn panics
scenarios.then: the histogram observation is still recorded via defer and the panic propagates unchanged
and_then: panic-safety is required because controller-runtime's recovery middleware would otherwise see missing metrics

requirements.description: Custom metrics SHALL be registered on controller-runtime's global metrics.Registry so they are served at the existing :8080/metrics endpoint
requirements.rationale: The manager already exposes /metrics; registering on the same registry avoids a second HTTP server and keeps the scrape contract stable with the already-configured Service port

scenarios.name: Metrics served at :8080/metrics
scenarios.when: the operator is running and an HTTP GET is made to http://<pod-ip>:8080/metrics
scenarios.then: the response body contains keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds HELP and TYPE comments
and_then: no secondary listener is opened on any other port

scenarios.name: Registration uses controller-runtime package
scenarios.when: init() of the metrics package runs
scenarios.then: it calls metrics.Registry.MustRegister from sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics
and_then: init() must be idempotent under test — use sync.Once or guard registration

requirements.description: The project SHALL ship reference documentation listing every exported metric with name, type, labels, buckets, and semantic meaning
requirements.rationale: Dashboard authors and alert authors need a stable contract; undocumented metric names become de-facto API surface and are harder to evolve

scenarios.name: Reference doc exists at documented location
scenarios.when: a reviewer checks docs/reference/keystone-operator-metrics.md
scenarios.then: the file exists, is linked from docs/guides/observability.md, and follows the existing reference-doc layout

scenarios.name: Doc matches emitted contract
scenarios.when: a unit test parses the metric names from the doc and compares them to registered collectors
scenarios.then: the two sets match exactly
and_then: this is a regression guard against silent renames

requirements.description: The project SHALL ship a starter Grafana dashboard JSON using only metrics defined in this feature
requirements.rationale: A usable dashboard day-one dramatically lowers the cost of adoption and prevents metric names from silently decoupling from downstream consumers

scenarios.name: Dashboard file parses as JSON
scenarios.when: a unit test unmarshals operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json into map[string]any
scenarios.then: parsing succeeds with no error

scenarios.name: Dashboard references only defined metrics
scenarios.when: a unit test extracts every expr field from the dashboard JSON and compares metric names against registered collectors
scenarios.then: every referenced metric is either registered by this feature or is a controller-runtime built-in
and_then: no dangling or speculative metric names

requirements.description: The project SHOULD ship a how-to guide for enabling operator metrics scraping end-to-end
requirements.rationale: Helm opt-in + dashboard import is the user-facing task flow; a how-to turns three reference docs into a 5-minute setup

scenarios.name: How-to exists and passes structure
scenarios.when: a reviewer opens docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md
scenarios.then: it follows Prerequisites → Steps → Verification → See Also
and_then: Verification section includes curl http://<service>:8080/metrics and expected line prefix

scenarios.name: How-to stays in sync with values
scenarios.when: a linter greps the how-to for "monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled"
scenarios.then: the key matches the real values.yaml path

requirements.description: Metric label cardinality SHALL NOT grow with the number of Keystone CRs for sub-reconciler-level metrics
requirements.rationale: Per-CR labels on high-frequency metrics (reconcile_duration, reconcile_errors) cause unbounded cardinality; Keystone name belongs only on low-frequency gauges (rotation age) and Job-transition counters (db_sync)

scenarios.name: Reconcile metrics omit CR name
scenarios.when: inspecting the label sets on keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds and keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total
scenarios.then: neither includes a keystone or namespace label
and_then: sub_reconciler and condition_type have bounded codomain drawn from subConditionTypes

scenarios.name: Low-frequency gauges are allowed per-CR labels
scenarios.when: inspecting keystone_operator_key_rotation_age_seconds and keystone_operator_db_sync_total
scenarios.then: these are labelled by keystone and namespace
and_then: scrape interval and update rate are low enough that the cardinality is acceptable for realistic cluster sizes (<1000 Keystone CRs)

tasks.title: Create operators/keystone/internal/metrics package with reconcile duration histogram, error counter, rotation age gauge, and db_sync counter/histogram; register on controller-runtime metrics.Registry via sync.Once; include a DeleteForKeystone(name, namespace) helper that calls gauge.DeleteLabelValues for per-CR series (CC-0089, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-008, REQ-012)

Write unit tests in operators/keystone/internal/metrics/collectors_test.go covering: histogram registration, counter increments only on err, gauge set+delete, unparseable-timestamp rejection, DeleteForKeystone, and label cardinality assertions (CC-0089, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-012)

Add operators/keystone/internal/metrics/cardinality_test.go asserting reconcile_duration and reconcile_errors descriptors contain no keystone/namespace labels — drift guard against accidental high-cardinality additions (CC-0089, REQ-012)

Add instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, name, fn) helper to operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation.go; use defer + time.Since to record duration; map name → condition_type via a package-private table built from subConditionTypes; call metrics.RecordReconcileError on non-nil err (CC-0089, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-007)

Add operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation_test.go with unit tests: records duration on success, records duration AND increments error counter on err, panic-safe (defer observes before re-panic), and lookup table covers every entry in subConditionTypes (CC-0089, REQ-007)

Refactor Reconcile in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go to wrap every sub-reconciler call (reconcileSecrets, reconcileDBConnectionSecret, reconcileConfig adapter, parallel group members, reconcileDatabase, reconcilePolicyValidation, reconcileDeployment, reconcileHTTPRoute, reconcileHealthCheck, reconcileHPA, reconcileBootstrap, reconcileTrustFlush) with instrumentSubReconciler — no behavioural change (CC-0089, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-007)

Extend reconcileParallelGroup to attribute errgroup errors back to the offending sub-reconciler name so RecordReconcileError is called with the correct sub_reconciler label — capture per-member error in the outcome struct and emit inside reconcileParallelGroup (CC-0089, REQ-002)

Add a test in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go (TestReconcileEmitsDurationForEverySubReconciler) asserting a single Reconcile pass emits duration samples for every sub_reconciler name (CC-0089, REQ-001)
Add a test (TestReconcileParallelGroupErrorCountsAreAttributed) injecting a CredentialKeys failure and asserting the error counter increments only for sub_reconciler=CredentialKeys (CC-0089, REQ-002)

In reconcile_fernet.go, after a successful ensureStagingSecret Get, read the rotation-completed-at annotation on the staging Secret; if present and parseable, call metrics.SetKeyRotationAge(keystone, namespace, "fernet", parsedTime); if absent/malformed, do NOT set the gauge (CC-0089, REQ-003)
Mirror 3.1 in reconcile_credential.go for key_type=credential (CC-0089, REQ-003)
Extend reconcile_fernet_test.go with TestFernetReconcileUpdatesRotationAgeGauge and an absent-annotation negative case; do the same in reconcile_credential_test.go (CC-0089, REQ-003)

In reconcile_database.go, on detecting a db_sync Job terminal transition (Complete=True or Failed=True), emit metrics.RecordDBSync(keystone, namespace, result, duration) where duration = condition.LastTransitionTime - Job.CreationTimestamp; guard with a transition-detection predicate so the same Job UID emits exactly once — use status annotation/field to remember last-observed Job UID (CC-0089, REQ-005)
Extend reconcile_database_test.go with TestDbSyncCompletionRecordsMetric and TestDbSyncFailureRecordsMetric plus a TestDbSyncInProgressDoesNotRecord case (CC-0089, REQ-005)

In reconcileDelete (keystone_controller.go), after RemoveFinalizer and successful Update, call metrics.DeleteForKeystone(keystone.Name, keystone.Namespace) so per-CR gauge series do not leak (CC-0089, REQ-004)
Add TestReconcileDeleteRemovesRotationAgeSeries to keystone_controller_test.go — seed the gauge, run reconcileDelete, assert series absence (CC-0089, REQ-004)

Add operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/servicemonitor.yaml rendering only when .Values.monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled; apiVersion monitoring.coreos.com/v1; selector uses keystone-operator.selectorLabels; endpoints[0] port=metrics, path=/metrics, interval from .Values.monitoring.serviceMonitor.interval default 30s (CC-0089, REQ-006)
Extend operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml with a monitoring block: monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=false (default) and monitoring.serviceMonitor.interval=30s (CC-0089, REQ-006)
Extend operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json with the monitoring block (enabled: boolean, interval: string with duration pattern) to fail-fast on bad inputs (CC-0089, REQ-006)

Add operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/servicemonitor_test.go with TestServiceMonitorRenderedWhenEnabled, TestServiceMonitorOmittedByDefault, TestValuesSchemaRejectsBadMonitoringInput using helm template + sigs.k8s.io/yaml parsing (CC-0089, REQ-006)

Add operators/keystone/dashboards/keystone-operator.json with panels: reconcile duration p95 per sub_reconciler (histogram_quantile on rate of _bucket), reconcile error rate per condition_type (rate of _total), rotation age in seconds per keystone+key_type, db_sync duration p95 and failure count (CC-0089, REQ-010)

Add operators/keystone/dashboards/dashboard_test.go with TestDashboardParsesAsJSON and TestDashboardReferencesOnlyRegisteredMetrics (extract metric names from panel.targets[].expr via regex, intersect with the registered set from the metrics package) (CC-0089, REQ-010)

Write docs/reference/keystone-operator-metrics.md — one table per metric with name, type, labels, buckets (for histograms), and semantic meaning; include ServiceMonitor values.yaml snippet; link from docs/guides/observability.md (CC-0089, REQ-009)

Add operators/keystone/internal/metrics/contract_test.go (TestReferenceDocMatchesRegisteredMetrics) that parses the reference doc and asserts every H2/backticked metric name matches a registered collector — regression guard (CC-0089, REQ-009)

Write docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-metrics.md — Prerequisites (prometheus-operator-crds installed), Steps (helm upgrade with monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true; import dashboards/keystone-operator.json in Grafana), Verification (curl :8080/metrics, sample PromQL), See Also (reference doc) (CC-0089, REQ-011)

Append a 'Metrics Instrumentation' section to docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md describing instrumentSubReconciler, the name→condition_type lookup, and the rule that every new sub-reconciler must be wrapped — pointer to 6.1 for metric details (CC-0089, REQ-007, REQ-009)

Add integration test TestMetricsEndpointServesKeystoneOperatorCollectors to operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go — envtest manager, create a Keystone CR, scrape http://<mgr.GetMetricsServer()>/metrics, assert keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds TYPE line is present (CC-0089, REQ-008)

Add integration test TestReconcileErrorsTotalIncrementsOnInducedFailure — envtest, create a Keystone CR whose database Secret is missing, run one reconcile cycle, assert keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total{sub_reconciler="Secrets"} >= 1 via the registry gather (CC-0089, REQ-002, REQ-008)

Add e2e test tests/e2e/keystone/metrics_test.go — chainsaw-driven: install chart with monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=true, assert ServiceMonitor exists, poll Prometheus API until keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds target is Up (CC-0089, REQ-006)

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator observes reconciliation health via Prometheus
test_specifications.expected: [unit] After init(), metrics.Registry contains a collector named keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds with documented buckets

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator observes reconciliation health via Prometheus
test_specifications.expected: [unit] keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total has exactly labels {sub_reconciler, condition_type} and increments correctly for a known pair

review_criteria: BLOCKER: keystone_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds observed for every sub-reconciler listed in subConditionTypes on a single end-to-end Reconcile (REQ-001)
BLOCKER: keystone_operator_reconcile_errors_total increments exactly once per errored sub-reconciler call, correctly attributed in the parallel group (REQ-002)
BLOCKER: No keystone/namespace label on reconcile_duration or reconcile_errors — cardinality drift guard passes (REQ-012)
BLOCKER: ServiceMonitor template is opt-in and defaults to disabled — helm template with default values renders zero ServiceMonitor objects (REQ-006)
BLOCKER: metrics.DeleteForKeystone is called in reconcileDelete after finalizer removal (REQ-004)
BLOCKER: db_sync metric emits exactly once per Job terminal-state transition, not per reconcile pass (REQ-005)
CRITICAL: Every sub-reconciler call site uses instrumentSubReconciler — no direct calls bypass instrumentation (REQ-007)
CRITICAL: rotation age gauge is NOT set when annotation is absent or unparseable — PromQL absent() remains the alert signal (REQ-003)
CRITICAL: All new code passes go vet, golangci-lint, and go test ./... with no //nolint suppressions beyond the pre-existing ones
CRITICAL: TestConditionTypeLookupCoversAllSubConditions passes — future sub-reconciler additions without lookup entries fail CI (REQ-007)
CRITICAL: Reference doc and dashboard JSON contain exactly the set of metric names registered by the collectors package — drift guards (tasks 5.2, 6.2) pass (REQ-009, REQ-010)
CRITICAL: Integration test asserts /metrics endpoint exposes keystone_operator_* metrics via envtest manager (REQ-008)
MINOR: how-to guide verification section includes a sample curl + expected prefix for :8080/metrics (REQ-011)
MINOR: Grafana dashboard uses consistent panel titles matching the metric names (REQ-010)
MINOR: values.schema.json rejects non-boolean for monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled at helm template time (REQ-006)
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS (CC-0089):

1) Registration target: controller-runtime's metrics.Registry (sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/metrics). This is the same registry served at :8080/metrics by the manager configured in internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go:128. No new HTTP listener is required.

2) Instrumentation layer: sub-reconciler calls are wrapped at the main Reconcile loop (keystone_controller.go:157-254) by a single helper instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, name, fn). This avoids editing every reconcile_*.go file and guarantees that a future sub-reconciler cannot forget instrumentation — the diff is one line at the call site.

3) Metric taxonomy and cardinality discipline (REQ-012):
   - HIGH-FREQUENCY, LOW-CARDINALITY: reconcile_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: sub_reconciler), reconcile_errors_total (counter, labels: sub_reconciler, condition_type). Bounded by subConditionTypes size (~12).
   - LOW-FREQUENCY, PER-CR: key_rotation_age_seconds (gauge, labels: keystone, namespace, key_type), db_sync_total (counter, labels: keystone, namespace, result), db_sync_duration_seconds (histogram, labels: keystone, namespace).
   - DELETE-ON-CR-DELETE: per-CR series are cleaned up in reconcileDelete via metrics.DeleteForKeystone to prevent unbounded growth over CR churn (REQ-004).

4) Sub-reconciler name → condition_type mapping: built from subConditionTypes (keystone_controller.go:49-62) plus the three const-declared types. The mapping lives in instrumentation.go and is verified by TestConditionTypeLookupCoversAllSubConditions to prevent drift — if anyone adds a new sub-reconciler without extending the lookup, the test fails at CI.

5) Rotation age derivation (REQ-003): the forge.c5c3.io/rotation-completed-at annotation on the staging Secret (constants in rotation_staging.go:30-33) is already parsed by applyRotationOutput. The gauge update is a side effect of a successful Get in reconcile_fernet.go / reconcile_credential.go — when the annotation is absent or unparseable, the gauge is deliberately NOT set so PromQL absent() becomes the alert signal (this is why gauge.Delete must be callable, not just a zero value).

6) db_sync one-shot emission (REQ-005): the challenge is that reconcileDatabase runs repeatedly while the Job runs. Two approaches considered:
   (a) compare observed Job condition against a memoised last-observed-UID+phase stored in Keystone status — chosen, because it survives operator restarts and is idempotent across requeues.
   (b) rely on controller-runtime watch-event dedup — rejected, because Job watches fire on every status patch (pod transitions, backoff), not only on terminal transitions.
   The predicate is simple: emit metric iff (Job.UID changed since last emission) OR (Job transitioned Complete/Failed since last emission).

7) Helm chart opt-in (REQ-006): ServiceMonitor is a Prometheus Operator CRD. Emitting unconditionally would break installation on clusters without prometheus-operator-crds. The values.yaml default of monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled=false preserves zero-impact upgrades for existing users. values.schema.json validation catches typos at helm lint time rather than at apply time.

8) Panic safety: instrumentSubReconciler uses defer + time.Since to record duration regardless of how fn exits (normal return, err return, panic). A panic re-propagates — recovery is controller-runtime's responsibility.

9) Testing strategy:
   - Unit: operators/keystone/internal/metrics/collectors_test.go exercises the collectors directly against a fresh prometheus.NewRegistry to avoid cross-test pollution.
   - Unit: operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation_test.go exercises the helper against a mock registry.
   - Unit: operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/servicemonitor_test.go uses helm template + sigs.k8s.io/yaml to assert rendered output — same pattern as existing chart tests if present, otherwise new.
   - Integration: operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go adds //go:build integration tests that hit the manager's metrics server via httptest-style client (mgr.GetMetricsServer() is available in controller-runtime v0.18+).
   - E2E: tests/e2e/keystone/metrics_test.go via chainsaw asserts the ServiceMonitor → Prometheus scrape chain works end-to-end with prometheus-operator-crds installed in the kind cluster (already available per deploy/flux-system/releases/prometheus-operator-crds.yaml).

ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM:

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ KeystoneReconciler.Reconcile (main loop)     │
  │                                              │
  │  for each sub-reconciler:                    │
  │   ─► instrumentSubReconciler(ctx, name, fn)  │
  │        │                                     │
  │        ├── defer: observe duration histogram │
  │        ├── run fn → (Result, err)            │
  │        └── if err: increment errors counter  │
  │                                              │
  │  reconcileFernet ── sets rotation_age gauge  │
  │  reconcileCredential ── sets rotation_age    │
  │  reconcileDatabase ── emits db_sync metrics  │
  │  reconcileDelete ── DeleteForKeystone(...)   │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
               │ register
               ▼
  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ controller-runtime metrics.Registry (global) │
  │   — served at :8080/metrics by manager       │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
               │ scrape
               ▼
  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ ServiceMonitor (opt-in, monitoring.*)        │
  │ ─► Prometheus Operator ─► Prometheus         │
  │                              │               │
  │                              ▼               │
  │                        Grafana dashboard     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘

DATA FLOW — ROTATION AGE:
  CronJob ──PATCH(data, annotation)──► staging Secret
                                         │
        reconcileFernet Get ◄────────────┘
                │ parse annotation RFC3339
                ▼
        metrics.SetKeyRotationAge(name, ns, 'fernet', parsedTime)
                ▼
        gauge = time.Since(parsedTime).Seconds()

KEY FILES:
  - operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go  (wrap sub-reconciler calls)
  - operators/keystone/internal/controller/rotation_staging.go:30-33  (annotation constants — reuse, do not redefine)
  - operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:53-79  (condition reason constants — align db_sync labels)
  - internal/common/bootstrap/manager.go:128-130  (do NOT modify — already configured correctly)
  - operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/service.yaml:19-22  (port name 'metrics' — ServiceMonitor must reference this exact name)
  - operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json  (extend with monitoring block)
  - architecture/docs/06-operations/02-observability/01-metrics.md:52-68  (read-only reference — ServiceMonitor pattern already documented; implementation must match)

PITFALLS:
  - Re-registering collectors during test runs panics; use prometheus.NewRegistry per-test for isolation.
  - parallelGroup errgroup.Wait returns the FIRST error only; to attribute errors to specific sub-reconcilers, capture per-member error in the outcome slice (task 2.4) BEFORE calling RecordReconcileError.
  - Deleting a gauge series for a label combination that was never set is a no-op — safe to always call on reconcileDelete.
  - helm template assertions must pin the Helm version; use the one already used by chart tests in the repo.
  - ServiceMonitor's selector.matchLabels MUST match keystone-operator.selectorLabels exactly (the same template used by the Service) or Prometheus will match no endpoints.
